name: python3-pipdeptree
title: python3-pipdeptree
summary: display dependency tree of the installed Python 3 packages
description:
display dependency tree of the installed Python 3 packages
Pipdeptree is a command line utility for displaying the installed Python
packages in form of a dependency tree. It works for packages installed
globally on a machine as well as in a virtualenv. Since pip freeze shows
all dependencies as a flat list, finding out which are the top level
packages and which packages do they depend on requires some effort. It
can also be tedious to resolve conflicting dependencies because pip
doesn't yet have true dependency resolution (more on this later). This
utility tries to solve this problem.
.
To some extent, this tool is inspired by lein deps :tree command of
Leiningen.

commands.name: pipdeptree
commands.description: display dependency tree of the installed Python 3 packages display dependency tree of the installed Python packages

arguments.description: list all deps at top level
arguments.raw: -a  list all deps at top level

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use pipdeptree because display dependency tree of the installed Python 3 packages.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use pipdeptree as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pipdeptree [-h] [-v] [-f] [-a] [-l] [-u] [-w [{silence,suppress,fail}]] [-r] [-p PACKAGES] [-e` as documented upstream for pipdeptree.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair pipdeptree with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `pipdeptree --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install python-pipdeptree. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `pipdeptree [-h] [-v] [-f] [-a] [-l] [-u] [-w [{silence,suppress,fail}]] [-r] [-p PACKAGES] [-e`. display dependency tree of the installed Python 3 packages.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: display dependency tree of the installed Python 3 packages.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: pipdeptree - display dependency tree of the installed Python 3 packages display dependency tree of the installed Python packages.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install python-pipdeptree
Install with: sudo apt install python-pipdeptree
